author: Dr. Phong Le, MD
department: Primary Care — Charm City Asian Health Center
content:
ANNUAL PRIMARY CARE VISIT — 10/22/2026, 9:00 AM
Patient: Nguyen, Lan Thi (Age 72)
Insurance: Medicare A+B + Maryland Medicaid (dual). WellCare Part D.
Vietnamese-language visit (no third-party interpreter).

INTERVAL HISTORY (since 2025 annual):
Ms. Nguyen is back for her annual visit. Major updates over the past year:
- BROTHER (the heavy smoker, then 64) DIED in March 2026 of his advanced squamous cell lung cancer. Family had been preparing; she traveled by phone for memorial. She has been processing the loss.
- Cholelithiasis: she had biliary colic episode 9/30/2025 (Hopkins Bayview UC). Referred to General Surgery (Dr. Markovich). She SAW the surgeon 11/14/2025 — he recommended laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Patient elected to DEFER given her concerns about surgical recovery and a general feeling of "don't fix what isn't urgently broken." Has had 1 mild similar episode in summer 2026 (resolved with rest + clear liquids overnight, no medical attention). She agrees if there is a more severe attack she will go in. Currently doing well on a low-fat diet.
- Cognitively/functionally she remains well; daily walks, tai chi 2x/week.
- No new symptoms today. Specifically denies chest pain, dyspnea, cough, hemoptysis, fever, night sweats, weight loss, syncope, GU symptoms, GI symptoms outside the prior biliary episodes, or new joint complaints. Left knee continues to bother her on stairs but manageable without medications.

ROS otherwise negative.

PMH (current):
- Hypertension (well-controlled)
- Hyperlipidemia (at goal)
- CKD stage 2/3a (Cr ~1.3, eGFR 41-47 trending)
- Cholelithiasis (symptomatic, surgery deferred by patient)
- s/p Right TKA 4/2018 (excellent outcome)
- Latent TB infection (positive PPD/IGRA 2010, untreated)
- Left knee mild OA
- Osteopenia
- Allergies: PCN childhood rash, iodinated contrast (pre-medication required)
- Post-menopausal

MEDS RECONCILED (with patient's son via phone, brought all bottles):
- Lisinopril 5 mg PO daily (dose was decreased from 10 to 5 in 1/2026 after her BP ran lower and Cr ticked up to 1.4 transiently; back to baseline since)
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily
- Acetaminophen 500-1000 mg PO PRN (mainstay analgesic)
- Ibuprofen 400-800 mg PO PRN — uses ~3-4x/week despite CKD counseling, mostly for left knee pain. She finds it more effective than acetaminophen.
- Calcium 600 mg + vitamin D3 1000 IU daily
- Multivitamin daily

Medication adherence: described as "good" — patient confirms she takes lisinopril and atorvastatin every morning. Has not refilled aspirin since we agreed to stop it in 2024 given USPSTF guidance and minimal vascular events.

FAMILY HISTORY UPDATE: Brother (older) — d. 3/2026 of lung cancer at 64.

VITALS: BP 136/80, HR 76, RR 14, T 97.8, SpO2 98% RA. Weight 62.0 kg, BMI 26.1.

EXAM:
General: Well-appearing 72-year-old woman, alert and engaged.
HEENT: Normal. Hearing slightly diminished but conversational.
CV: RRR, no murmur. No JVD. Peripheral pulses 2+ symmetric.
Lungs: CTAB. No wheezes, no crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, NT/ND. Murphy sign negative (asymptomatic today). No organomegaly. Normal bowel sounds.
MSK: Right knee — well-healed scar, no effusion, full ROM. Left knee — mild medial tenderness, no effusion, full ROM with crepitus.
Skin: No suspicious lesions on full-skin survey.
Neuro: Cognitively intact (MoCA in Vietnamese not formally done today; clinical impression normal).

LABS (drawn today, results back same week):
- BMP: Na 139, K 4.2, Cl 102, CO2 24, BUN 24, Cr 1.3 (eGFR 47), Glu 99
- LFTs: AST 28, ALT 26, ALP 78, T-bili 0.7, Alb 4.0 (all normal)
- LIPID PANEL: TC 162, LDL 78 (at goal), HDL 52, TG 130
- HbA1c 5.8 (pre-diabetic range — stable from last 2 years; lifestyle counseling continued)
- TSH 2.92 (normal)
- CBC: WBC 5.8, Hgb 11.6 (mildly low, chronic; iron studies last year showed adequate iron, low normal range — likely anemia of chronic disease from CKD), Plt 232
- UA: trace protein, no blood. Spot ACR ordered.
- Vitamin D 28 (mildly low; will repeat on supplementation)

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. HTN — well-controlled on reduced-dose lisinopril 5 mg. Continue.
2. HLD — at goal. Continue atorvastatin 40 mg.
3. CKD stage 3a — stable today (Cr 1.3, eGFR 47). Re-emphasized minimizing NSAIDs. Patient acknowledged using ibuprofen too often; agrees to attempt to substitute with acetaminophen and try a topical diclofenac gel for left knee.
   → Prescribed diclofenac 1% gel topical TID PRN.
   → Repeat BMP + ACR in 4 months.
4. CHOLELITHIASIS — patient has elected non-surgical management; understands ongoing risk. Reinforced strict low-fat diet and immediate ED return for severe attack. Will continue to monitor.
5. LATENT TB INFECTION — declined treatment again. Documented (8th time over 16 years).
6. LUNG CANCER SCREENING — important conversation today. Patient brought this up unprompted, in the context of her brother's recent death. We had detailed discussion via interpreter line for fidelity:
   • She does not meet 2021 USPSTF criteria (14 lifetime pack-years; quit 22 years ago).
   • Her family history of lung cancer in a heavy-smoker brother is concerning, though brother's risk was largely driven by his own smoking, not heritable.
   • Low-dose chest CT screening is not covered by Medicare in her case (does not meet USPSTF). Out-of-pocket cost ~$250 at Hopkins.
   • Shared decision making: she would benefit from a baseline LDCT given anxiety + family loss + minor smoking + her age, but cost is real.
   • She AGAIN DECLINED LDCT today — primarily a cost concern. We documented this carefully and discussed she may revisit if she develops symptoms (cough, hemoptysis) or if her financial situation changes.
7. FALLS — single fall reported in past 12 months (slipped on icy stoop, no injury). Discussed home safety. Provided home-safety handout in Vietnamese.
8. ADVANCE CARE PLANNING — discussed; she has not formally completed an advance directive. She verbally identifies her son Quang as the person who would make decisions if she could not. We provided MOLST forms in Vietnamese for her to discuss with family; will revisit at next visit.
9. HEALTH MAINTENANCE — flu shot today; mammogram (last 8/2025, negative); colonoscopy due 2029; DEXA last 2024 (osteopenia stable).

Follow-up: 4 months for BMP + ACR + lipid recheck. Annual physical 10/2027.

Visit time: 65 minutes.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Phong Le, MD
Charm City Asian Health Center
Date: 10/22/2026
